regimeSub: Inflation re-accelerating to 4.2% as Warsh's Fed kills cut bias and pivots to hikes, even with oil cratering on the Iran deal.
themes: US-Iran deal sinks Brent to 3-month lows -> oil tailwind, but core inflation still sticky at 4.2%
Warsh Fed strips cutting bias and signals hikes -> bond yields surge, Dow drops 500
Negative real policy rate (3.63% vs 4.2% CPI) -> Fed behind the curve, gold bid to $4,339
Soft 1.6% GDP plus rising prices -> stagflation squeeze, equity breadth narrowing as semis roll over

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy rate 3.63% vs 4.2% CPI = negative real; Fed forced to lean hawkish to catch up.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +38bps and steepening as Warsh's hike signal lifts the long end; term premium rebuilding.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Still contained; no credit event yet, but hawkish Fed plus soft GDP is the risk to watch.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 18.4 — elevated but orderly; absorbing Fed hawkish shock and Iran-deal relief simultaneously.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $78, 3-month lows on US-Iran agreement; disinflation help but EIA draws cap the downside.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4,339 near record — hedging negative real rates and Fed credibility under new chair Warsh.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1,522 — won deeply weak; high US rates and strong-ish DXY pressure Asian FX.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: Nvidia/Broadcom/Micron led Nasdaq drop; AI leadership wobbling as yields spike.

headline: Oil has collapsed on an Iran deal but US inflation just hit a 3-year high at 4.2%, and Warsh's Fed has flipped from cuts to hikes — markets now price stagflation, not soft landing.
